title: Authors and Citation
text: Calderan M (2026). multibayes: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/mar-cald/multibayes.
code: @Manual{,   title = {multibayes: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments},   author = {Margherita Calderan},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/mar-cald/multibayes}, }

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: multibayes provides tools Bayesian multiple testing, currently centred prior-odds adjustment Probability Direction (pd). direction-agnostic directional tests supported, can mixed freely within single call.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: Probability Direction (pd) often used decision rule: hypothesis accepted pd exceeds threshold. decision made repeatedly across several hypotheses, family-wise error probability can much higher intended. pd.adjust() addresses problem making implicit prior probability global null explicit, following prior-odds framework Jeffreys (1938) Westfall et al. (1997). global prior probability hypotheses null, q, decomposed per-hypothesis prior: P(H0)=q1/mP(H_0) = q^{1/m} pd reweighted Bayes’ theorem: pdadj=pd⋅P(H1)pd⋅P(H1)+(1−pd)⋅P(H0)pd_{\text{adj}} = \frac{pd \cdot P(H_1)}{pd \cdot P(H_1) + (1 - pd) \cdot P(H_0)} prior conservative (P(H0)>P(H1)P(H_0) > P(H_1)), adjustment always shrinks pd toward lower bound. parameters correlated, effective number tests meffm_\text{eff} (Cheverud, 2001) used place m, producing less conservative adjustment. function supports two testing modes, can mixed across hypotheses within call: Direction-agnostic (direction = 0): pd = max(Pr(θ̂>θnull),Pr(θ̂<θnull))\max\!\big(\Pr(\hat\theta > \theta_\text{null}),\, \Pr(\hat\theta < \theta_\text{null})\big), bounded [0.5,1][0.5, 1] construction; pdadjpd_\text{adj} also floored 0.50.5. Directional (direction = 1 -1): pd raw one-sided posterior probability predicted side, [0,1][0, 1], following Westfall et al. (1997). Values 0.50.5 indicate posterior concentrated opposite predicted direction; adjustment shrink values, reflecting combined weight data conservative prior hypothesis.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
code: library(multibayes)  # From a vector of pd values (independence assumed, direction-agnostic) pd_values <- c(H1 = 0.999, H2 = 0.946, H3 = 0.813, H4 = 0.763, H5 = 0.891, H6 = 0.987) pd.adjust(pd = pd_values, q = 0.4)  # Simulate correlated posterior draws Sigma <- matrix(0.4, nrow = 6, ncol = 6); diag(Sigma) <- 1 mu    <- c(1, -0.1, 0.8, 0, 2, 3) draws <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = 4000, mu = mu, Sigma = Sigma) colnames(draws) <- c("H1", "H2", "H3", "H4", "H5", "H6")  # From posterior draws: pd and correlation estimated automatically pd.adjust(draws = draws, q = 0.4, mu0 = 0, R = TRUE)  # Mix of directional and agnostic tests with parameter-specific nulls pd.adjust(draws = draws, q = 0.4, mu0 = c(0.2, 0, 0.2, 0, 0.5, 0.5),           direction = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1), R = TRUE)  # When draws are unavailable, supply an assumed mean correlation pd.adjust(pd = pd_values, q = 0.4, R = 0.4)

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: draws supplied, output data.frame one row per hypothesis: pd vector supplied directly, pd, pd_adj, q, m returned.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: q encodes prior belief tested hypotheses simultaneously null. value 0.4 skeptical default. mu0 sets null reference value parameter. scalar applies null parameters; vector allows different null per parameter, instance testing minimum effect practical interest. direction selects testing mode per hypothesis (1 positive, -1 negative, 0 agnostic). scalar recycled; mixed vector applies different modes across hypotheses within call. directional tests, values pd pd_adj 0.5 indicate data contradicted predicted direction; adjustment amplifies evidence conservative prior.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: use multibayes published research, please cite: Calderan, M., Gambarota, F., Toffalini, E., & Altoè, G. (2026). Adjusting Probability Direction Multiple Testing.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: Jeffreys, H. (1938). Significance tests several degrees freedom arise simultaneously. Proceedings Royal Society London. Series . Mathematical Physical Sciences, 165(921), 161–198. https://doi.org/10.1098/rspa.1938.0052 Westfall, P. H., Johnson, W. O., & Utts, J. M. (1997). Bayesian perspective Bonferroni adjustment. Biometrika, 84(2), 419–427. https://doi.org/10.2307/2337467 Cheverud, J. (2001). simple correction multiple comparisons interval mapping genome scans. Heredity, 87, 52–58. https://doi.org/10.1046/j.1365-2540.2001.00901.x

what: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: function accepts either vector pre-computed pd values matrix posterior draws, pd values computed internally. direction-agnostic directional tests supported: direction argument controls formulation applied per hypothesis. global prior probability tested hypotheses null, \(q\), decomposed per-hypothesis prior \(P(H_0) = q^{1/m}\), \(m\) number hypotheses , correlation structure among parameters taken account, effective number tests \(m_{\text{eff}}\).

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
code: pd.adjust(   pd = NULL,   draws = NULL,   q = 0.4,   mu0 = 0,   direction = NULL,   R = NULL )

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: pd Numeric vector pd values. direction-agnostic tests, values must \([0.5, 1]\). directional tests, values raw one-sided probabilities \([0, 1]\). Ignored draws supplied. draws Optional matrix data frame posterior draws (columns = parameters). provided, pd values computed automatically draws according direction mu0. q Numeric scalar \((0, 1)\). prior probability hypotheses null simultaneously. Defaults 0.4. mu0 Numeric scalar vector. null (reference) value posterior evaluated. scalar applies null parameters; vector length equal ncol(draws) allows different null per parameter. Ignored pd supplied directly. Defaults 0. direction Integer vector 1, -1, 0 (NULL). Specifies testing mode hypothesis: 1 positive directional test (\(\Pr(\theta > \theta_\text{null})\)), -1 negative directional test (\(\Pr(\theta < \theta_\text{null})\)), 0 direction-agnostic testing (maximum sides). scalar recycled match number parameters; mixed vectors allow different modes across hypotheses. Defaults NULL (direction-agnostic parameters). R Optional correlation information computing \(m_{\text{eff}}\). Accepts TRUE (correlation estimated draws), numeric scalar (assumed uniform correlation applied parameter pairs), full correlation matrix. provided, \(m_{\text{eff}}\) replaces nominal \(m\).

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: data.frame one row per hypothesis, containing: pd (values used adjustment), pd_adj (adjusted values), q (prior probability global null), m (nominal effective number tests). direction-agnostic tests, pd pd_adj bounded \([0.5, 1]\); directional tests, \([0, 1]\), values \(0.5\) indicating data (adjustment) favoured opposite direction. draws supplied, output additionally includes mean_est (posterior mean per parameter), mu0 (null reference values), direction.

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: adjustment follows Bayes' theorem. Given per-hypothesis prior \(P(H_0) = q^{1/m}\) complement \(P(H_1) = 1 - P(H_0)\), adjusted pd : $$    pd_{adj} = \frac{pd \cdot P(H_1)}{pd \cdot P(H_1) + (1 - pd) \cdot P(H_0)} $$ prior conservative (\(P(H_0) > P(H_1)\)), adjustment always shrinks pd toward lower bound. Direction-agnostic tests (direction = 0): pd defined \(\max\!\big(\Pr(\hat\theta > \theta_\text{null}),\, \Pr(\hat\theta < \theta_\text{null})\big)\) bounded \([0.5, 1]\) construction. \(pd_{adj}\) also floored \(0.5\), adjustment produces shrinkage toward \(0.5\). Directional tests (direction = 1 -1): pd raw posterior probability mass predicted side, \(\Pr(\hat\theta > \theta_\text{null})\) \(\Pr(\hat\theta < \theta_\text{null})\), defined \([0, 1]\). follows directly Westfall et al. (1997), derived prior-odds correction one-sided hypotheses. Values pd \(0.5\) indicate posterior concentrated opposite predicted direction; adjustment shrink values toward \(0\), reflecting combined weight data conservative prior hypothesis. Mixed use directional direction-agnostic tests within call supported: element direction handled independently, prior-odds adjustment applied uniformly across hypotheses regardless directionality. R supplied, effective number tests \(m_{\text{eff}}\) estimated eigenvalues \(\lambda\) correlation matrix (Cheverud, 2001): $$    m_{\text{eff}} = K \left( 1 - \frac{(K-1)\,\text{Var}(\lambda)}{K^2} \right) $$ \(K\) number hypotheses.

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust

Biometrika, 84(2), 419–427. https://doi.org/10.2307/2337467 Cheverud, J. (2001). simple correction multiple comparisons interval mapping genome scans.
